source_record.title: Identification of resistance pathways and therapeutic targets in relapsed multiple myeloma patients through single-cell sequencing
source_record.study_context: Nature Medicine prospective multicenter translational trial combining DARA-KRD treatment context with longitudinal single-cell RNA sequencing in primary refractory or early relapsed multiple myeloma after bortezomib-containing induction; public extraction uses PubMed, Nature, and PubMed Central public source context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports single-cell transcriptional programs and molecular pathways associated with resistant multiple myeloma, including hypoxia tolerance, protein folding, and mitochondrial respiration signals, supporting residual or resistant state extraction as a biological layer separate from MRD measurement trajectory.
measurement_fields: cycle 4 and cycle 10 longitudinal sample context
limitations: Source-specific resistant-state signal in a translational-trial context.
Does not make single-cell transcriptional states routine clinical decision support.
Does not recommend a pathway, drug, target, treatment sequence, or monitoring plan.
Does not provide patient-specific MRD interpretation, prognosis, or cure evidence.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source includes transcriptional clone-level data and longitudinal malignant plasma cell state analysis, supporting a narrow clone-identity-to-transcriptional-state coupling slice for the geometry while preserving genetic architecture and nongenetic state as separate fields.
limitations: This signal supports clone-state coupling only within the source-defined single-cell and transcriptional-clone context.
It does not establish general genetic causality or rank genetic versus nongenetic mechanisms.
It does not identify patient-specific resistance biology or clinical actionability.
Cross-source comparison must preserve clone inference method, transcriptional-state method, therapy exposure, and sampling time point.

what_this_supports: Phase 2 can treat residual transcriptional adaptation as a source-backed biological state axis rather than folding it into MRD measurement trajectory.
Phase 2 can represent clone-state coupling as an explicit edge while keeping clone identity, transcriptional state, therapy exposure, assay method, and sampling time point separate.
The high-priority residual-state and clone-state coupling tasks can be marked done for v0 navigation after coverage is refreshed.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not provide medical advice, diagnostic guidance, treatment guidance, monitoring guidance, trial guidance, or patient-specific prognosis.
It does not rank resistance mechanisms or assert that any pathway is dominant across all multiple myeloma settings.
It does not claim that PPIA, protein folding, mitochondrial respiration, hypoxia tolerance, or any other extracted pathway is clinically actionable for an individual.
It does not claim that multiple myeloma has been cured.

review_notes: Built as the Phase 2 second-source extraction for residual transcriptional adaptation and clone-state coupling buckets.
Source details checked against PubMed, Nature Medicine, and PubMed Central public records for PMID 33619369 / PMCID PMC7612793.
The extraction keeps clone-state coupling as research-map structure and does not upgrade source observations into clinical actionability.
